stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/NLP tags including 'transformers', 'llama', and 'text-generation'.
This indicates active community interest and benchmarking of a significant large language model (LLM).

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating its use in large language model applications.
The model is associated with key AI technologies like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: Google ordered to put clearer links in AI search and let UK publishers opt out
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: Google has been legally compelled to improve the transparency of its AI Overviews.
The ruling mandates clearer sourcing links within Google's AI-generated search results.
UK publishers are granted the option to opt out of Google's AI indexing/display.

stories.title: Gemma 4 12B: A unified, encoder-free multimodal model
stories.author: rvz
bullet_points: Introduction of 'Gemma 4 12B', a new large language model.
The model features a unified and encoder-free multimodal architecture.
The announcement suggests advancements in efficient, integrated AI model design.

stories.title: Full Disclosure: 1-Click GitHub Token Stealing via a VSCode Bug
stories.author: blog.ammaraskar.com by ammar2
bullet_points: A critical vulnerability exists in VSCode that allows for one-click GitHub token theft.
The exploit targets GitHub tokens, representing a severe security risk for developers and organizations.
The disclosure highlights the importance of rigorous security auditing in developer tools.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under text-generation and conversational AI pipelines.
The associated tags indicate relevance to transformers, safetensors, and DeepSeek architectures.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags like 'transformers' and 'pytorch'.
The pipeline indicates the model is being used for text generation tasks.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated MLX for enhanced performance.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, optimizing AI workloads for Apple hardware.
The feature is currently available in preview, signaling a push for faster local AI inference on macOS.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs on the user's own device.
The core value proposition is providing private, on-device AI assistance for coding tasks.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, using local or cloud infrastructure.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is partnering with OpenAI and ROOST to integrate gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models.
These models are designed for safety classification tasks and are available in 20B and 120B sizes.
The gpt-oss-safeguard models are permissively licensed under the Apache 2.0 license.

stories.title: Nvidia is already planning N2X and N3X chips — the goal is the Star Trek computer
stories.author: Sean Hollister
bullet_points: Nvidia is planning future chip generations (N2X and N3X) beyond the initial RTX Spark.
The company intends for RTX Spark to be a sustained product line, not a one-off experiment.
CEO Jensen Huang confirmed the multi-generational roadmap at Computex 2026.

stories.title: Amazon’s search bar will invent AI-generated products you can’t buy
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Amazon is integrating an AI-powered feature into its search bar.
The feature generates AI images of products based on user descriptions.
Currently, the AI image generation is limited to clothing and home goods, serving as a visual search aid.

stories.title: Google's new Gemma 4 12B model is designed to run on any laptop with 16GB of RAM
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google has released the Gemma 4 12B model.
The model is optimized for local deployment, specifically targeting laptops with 16GB of RAM.
Performance improvements are attributed to a new encoding scheme and token prediction methodology.

stories.title: Autonomous vehicles were supposed to cut traffic—what if they don't?
stories.author: Jonathan M. Gitlin
bullet_points: Waymo's autonomous robotaxis are frequently driving empty.
The data indicates that nearly half of the miles driven by these vehicles are without passengers.
The finding challenges the initial premise that autonomous vehicles will inherently solve traffic congestion.

stories.title: Audio Interaction Model
stories.author: Chunyan Miao
bullet_points: Proposes the 'Audio Interaction Model' to unify current siloed audio AI systems (like offline LALMs and single-task streaming models).
The core concept is an 'always-on perceive-decide-respond loop' allowing real-time interaction with sound and environment.
Introduces 'Audio-Interaction' as a realization of this unified, online LALM framework.

stories.title: Who Needs Labels? Adapting Vision Foundation Models With the Metadata You Already Have
stories.author: Camille Couprie
bullet_points: Proposes FINO, a label-free method to adapt large vision foundation models to specialized scientific domains.
Addresses the limitations of standard supervised fine-tuning where labels are scarce and task-specific training risks losing model generality.
Leverages existing metadata in a self-supervised manner to guide representation adaptation to new domains.

stories.title: Audio Interaction Model
stories.author: Chunyan Miao
bullet_points: Proposes the 'Audio Interaction Model' to unify current fragmented audio models.
The goal is to create a single, online Large Audio Language Model (LALM) capable of real-time interaction.
This new model operates via a continuous 'perceive-decide-respond' loop, integrating sound, environment, and instructions.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, offering an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is the ability to run these AI agents locally on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, a popular local LLM runner.

stories.title: Subagents and web search in Claude Code
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has integrated support for subagents within Claude Code.
The update also enables web search functionality within the Claude Code environment.
This enhances the capabilities of AI agents by adding complex task decomposition and real-time information retrieval.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local model deployment and proprietary API capabilities.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex, when integrated, can read, modify, and execute code within a user's directory.
The system supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Nintendo confirms it will sell a new Switch 2 with replaceable battery in the EU
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Nintendo is planning to release the Switch 2 in the EU with a replaceable battery option.
This change is a direct response to upcoming EU regulations set to take effect in February 2027.
The move signals a shift toward greater user repairability in consumer electronics.

stories.title: Apple is bringing age verification to Texas this week
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Apple is implementing age verification within the App Store specifically for users in Texas.
This rollout is directly tied to the recent legal allowance of Texas' App Store Accountability Act.
The implementation begins on Thursday, June 4th, despite ongoing legal challenges to the state law.

stories.title: The best Qi2 batteries for iPhone and Pixel
stories.author: Cameron Faulkner
bullet_points: Qi2.2 certification enables faster wireless charging for modern iPhones (iPhone 16+).
New compact power banks can deliver up to 25W wireless charging speeds.
The integration of MagSafe magnetic snapping enhances usability and convenience.

stories.title: As AI gets better, it reveals an empty promise
stories.author: TC. Sottek
bullet_points: The article reviews Google's new Gemini AI agent, named Spark.
Hands-on tests suggest Spark is highly effective, noting its ability to recall personal details.
The review frames the AI's effectiveness as both impressive and potentially concerning ('scary').

stories.title: PlayStation is getting back to what it’s good at
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: PlayStation is shifting its development focus away from live-service games.
The console manufacturer is prioritizing premium, narrative-driven, single-player titles.
This strategic pivot was highlighted during the recent State of Play showcase.

stories.title: Can't make sense of Dashlane's vault theft notification? You're not alone.
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: Dashlane experienced a vault theft incident, prompting a security advisory.
The advisory provided insufficient detail regarding the nature of the breach.
Dashlane has maintained silence despite public confusion and concern over the incident.

stories.title: Inside Meta's attempts to play catch-up with AI
stories.author: Hannah Murphy, Financial Times
bullet_points: The article discusses Meta's ongoing efforts to improve its Artificial Intelligence capabilities.
There is expressed skepticism regarding Meta's ability to match the advancements of its competitors.
The piece focuses on the competitive landscape within the AI sector.

stories.title: Streaming Communication in Multi-Agent Reasoning
stories.author: Ying-Cong Chen
bullet_points: Addresses the latency bottleneck in multi-agent reasoning systems caused by sequential 'generate-then-transfer' paradigms.
Introduces StreamMA, a system that streams reasoning steps to downstream agents immediately, enabling pipeline parallelism.
Demonstrates that this pipelining not only reduces latency but also improves overall reasoning effectiveness.

stories.title: Reinforcement Learning from Rich Feedback with Distributional DAgger
stories.author: Paria Rashidinejad
bullet_points: The paper addresses the limitation of standard Reinforcement Learning from Verifiable Rewards (RLVR), which relies only on binary correctness feedback.
It proposes a novel approach by adapting the DAgger imitation learning algorithm to utilize 'rich feedback' (e.g., execution traces, tool outputs, expert corrections).
This work aims to improve reasoning models by effectively incorporating detailed, multi-faceted feedback beyond simple pass/fail signals.

stories.title: Failed Reasoning Traces Tell You What Is Fixable (But Not by Reading Them)
stories.author: Eilif B. Muller
bullet_points: The paper addresses the common practice of simply increasing compute for failed language model reasoning attempts.
It argues that failed inference traces contain crucial information about whether a failure is due to sampling luck or a fundamental structural issue.
The authors propose that these traces encode a 'recoverability structure' to guide effective intervention strategies.

stories.title: GeM-NR: Geometry-Aware Multi-View Editing for Nonrigid Scene Changes
stories.author: Fredrik Kahl
bullet_points: The paper introduces GeM-NR, a method for geometry-aware multi-view editing specifically designed for nonrigid scene changes.
Existing multi-view editing methods are often limited to rigid edits or appearance-only modifications, failing to capture complex scene structure changes.
This work advances the field toward more general 3D content generation and customization by addressing the limitations of current editing techniques.

stories.title: Towards Efficient and Evidence-grounded Mobility Prediction with LLM-Driven Agent
stories.author: Hiroki Kobayashi
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of individual-level mobility prediction, crucial for urban planning and simulation.
Proposes a new training-free LLM-driven agent to improve upon existing methods that lack decision-level transparency or struggle with weak data.
Focuses on enhancing LLM-based prediction by enabling the model to seek additional evidence when mobility signals are ambiguous.

stories.title: Continual Visual and Verbal Learning Through a Child's Egocentric Input
stories.author: Mengye Ren
bullet_points: Introduces BabyCL, a continual multimodal learning framework designed to mimic how children learn.
Addresses a key limitation in previous AI models that required cycling through shuffled data, contrasting with natural, chronological experience.
Applies to learning word-referent mappings from egocentric video data (SAYCam dataset).

stories.title: Arithmetic Pedagogy for Language Models
stories.author: Hokky Situngkir
bullet_points: The research explores applying human mathematics pedagogy (specifically the GASING method) to improve language model arithmetic reasoning.
The core methodology involves operationalizing arithmetic operations as computational procedures and serializing their execution traces into Chain-of-Thought (CoT) supervision.
The study demonstrates this approach using a relatively small GPT-2 decoder (86M parameters).

stories.title: STRIDE: Training Data Attribution via Sparse Recovery from Subset Perturbations
stories.author: Zhijing Jin
bullet_points: The paper addresses Training Data Attribution (TDA), the challenge of tracing model predictions back to their training data.
It proposes STRIDE, a method that uses sparse recovery from subset perturbations to approximate causal interventions without full retraining.
The work aims to overcome the computational expense of traditional TDA methods, especially for large models like LLMs.

stories.title: Reinforcement Learning from Rich Feedback with Distributional DAgger
stories.author: Paria Rashidinejad
bullet_points: The paper addresses the limitation of standard Reinforcement Learning from Verifiable Rewards (RLVR), which relies only on binary (correct/incorrect) feedback.
It proposes using a distributional variant of the DAgger algorithm to effectively leverage 'rich feedback' sources.
Rich feedback includes execution traces, tool outputs, expert corrections, and model self-evaluations, allowing for more nuanced learning.

stories.title: An Open-Source Two-Stage Computer Vision Pipeline for Fine-Grained Vehicle Classification using Vision Transformers
stories.author: Fred Feng
bullet_points: Introduces an open-source, two-stage computer vision pipeline for fine-grained vehicle classification.
Addresses a critical gap: current tools lack the ability to classify vehicles into injury-risk-relevant categories from real-world video.
The system aims to overcome limitations of standard object detection (coarse labels) and controlled-environment fine-grained recognition.

stories.title: Failed Reasoning Traces Tell You What Is Fixable (But Not by Reading Them)
stories.author: Eilif B. Muller
bullet_points: The paper addresses the common practice of simply increasing compute for LLM reasoning failures without analyzing the failure traces.
It proposes that failed reasoning traces contain a crucial signal about the 'recoverability structure' of the model's failure.
Failures are categorized into those solvable by more sampling (unlucky) and those that are structural and require different interventions.

stories.title: Activation-Based Active Learning for In-Context Learning: Challenges and Insights
stories.author: Stuart E. Middleton
bullet_points: The research proposes using transformer model activations as a fine-grained signal to optimize the selection of in-context examples for LLMs.
It addresses a gap in deep active learning for LLMs by integrating recent insights into transformer activation understanding.
The paper provides a comprehensive analysis of MLP activation-based deep active learning methods applied to in-context learning.

stories.title: Deep Embedded Multiplicative DMD for Algebra-Preserving Koopman Learning
stories.author: Matthew J. Colbrook
bullet_points: The research introduces Deep Embedded Multiplicative DMD, a novel method for Koopman learning.
Koopman theory aims to linearize complex nonlinear dynamics by transforming them into a spectral problem.
The method bridges the gap between flexible, deep learning coordinate discovery and structure-preserving methods that enforce operator identities.

stories.title: Towards Efficient and Evidence-grounded Mobility Prediction with LLM-Driven Agent
stories.author: Hiroki Kobayashi
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of individual-level mobility prediction, a key area in urban planning and transportation.
Proposes a novel, training-free LLM-driven agent to improve mobility prediction, moving beyond static prompt limitations.
Aims to enhance decision-level transparency and allow the model to seek additional evidence when mobility signals are weak or conflicting.

stories.title: Preserving Data Privacy in Learning Causal Structure with Fully Homomorphic Encryption
stories.author: Xiaofang Zhou
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of privacy leakage in distributed causal structure learning.
Proposes a novel method utilizing Fully Homomorphic Encryption (FHE) for computation on encrypted data.
Aims to maintain data privacy during both data transmission and computation phases.

stories.title: STRIDE: Training Data Attribution via Sparse Recovery from Subset Perturbations
stories.author: Zhijing Jin
bullet_points: The research addresses Training Data Attribution (TDA), the challenge of tracing LLM predictions back to their training data.
Traditional TDA methods using causal interventions are computationally infeasible for large models due to the need for repeated retraining.
The proposed method, STRIDE, offers a more efficient approximation of TDA by utilizing sparse recovery from subset perturbations, avoiding full gradient tracking.

stories.title: Beyond Text Following: Repairable Arbitration Reversals in Audio-Language Models
stories.author: Daling Wang
bullet_points: The research addresses a critical failure mode in Audio-Language Models (ALMs) where models prioritize conflicting text over clear audio evidence.
The study uses a 'same-audio counterfactual' method to test whether the correct audio-supported answer is being overridden by textual input.
Results across five ALMs and four conflict tasks indicate that a significant majority (64.1%) of conflict samples exhibit the problematic behavior.

stories.title: Streaming Communication in Multi-Agent Reasoning
stories.author: Ying-Cong Chen
bullet_points: Addresses the latency issue in multi-agent reasoning systems, which typically suffer from linear scaling with pipeline depth.
Introduces StreamMA, a novel system that streams reasoning steps to downstream agents immediately upon generation.
Demonstrates that this pipelining approach not only reduces latency but also surprisingly improves overall reasoning effectiveness.

stories.title: Reinforcement Learning from Rich Feedback with Distributional DAgger
stories.author: Paria Rashidinejad
bullet_points: The paper addresses the limitation of current Reinforcement Learning from Verifiable Rewards (RLVR), which relies only on binary (correct/incorrect) feedback.
It proposes using a distributional variant of the DAgger algorithm to effectively leverage 'rich feedback' sources.
Rich feedback includes detailed data like execution traces, tool outputs, expert corrections, and model self-evaluations.

stories.title: Failed Reasoning Traces Tell You What Is Fixable (But Not by Reading Them)
stories.author: Eilif B. Muller
bullet_points: The paper addresses the common practice of simply increasing compute when large language models fail on reasoning tasks.
It posits that failed inference traces contain crucial information about whether a failure is due to sampling luck or a fundamental structural issue.
The research proposes using these traces to determine the 'recoverability structure' of the model's failures, suggesting targeted interventions rather than brute-force computation.

stories.title: Activation-Based Active Learning for In-Context Learning: Challenges and Insights
stories.author: Stuart E. Middleton
bullet_points: The research proposes using transformer model activations to guide active learning for selecting in-context examples in LLMs.
It addresses a gap in existing deep active learning methods by integrating recent insights into transformer activation understanding.
The paper offers a comprehensive analysis of MLP activation-based deep active learning applied to in-context learning.

stories.title: Self-Evaluation Is Already There: Eliciting Latent Judge Calibration in Base LLMs with Minimal Data
stories.author: Zhenkai Liang
bullet_points: Base Large Language Models (LLMs) inherently possess a latent ability to predict external judge scores without specific fine-tuning.
The research introduces 'Self-Evaluation Elicitation' (SEE) to surface and leverage this pre-existing self-assessment capability.
The method demonstrates that few-shot prompting is sufficient for base models to accurately predict multi-attribute quality scores across benchmarks.

stories.title: Continual Visual and Verbal Learning Through a Child's Egocentric Input
stories.author: Mengye Ren
bullet_points: Introduces BabyCL, a continual multimodal learning framework for AI.
Addresses the inefficiency of current methods (shuffled data) by processing data in a single chronological pass, mimicking child learning.
Focuses on enabling neural networks to learn word-referent mappings from egocentric video input, similar to human children.

stories.title: Evaluating Large Language Models in Dynamic Clinical Decision-Making with Standardized Patient Cases
stories.author: Weidi Xie
bullet_points: The paper addresses the limitation of static benchmarks for evaluating LLMs in real-world clinical settings.
It proposes using standardized patient (SP) cases, traditionally used in medical education, to simulate dynamic clinical encounters.
The goal is to test how LLMs handle longitudinal care, including information gathering, treatment planning, and adaptation across patient states.

stories.title: I built a vulnerable app and spent $1,500 seeing if LLMs could hack it
stories.author: jc4p
bullet_points: A user built a deliberately vulnerable application.
The experiment tested the capability of Large Language Models (LLMs) to perform hacking/exploitation.
The project involved a financial investment of $1,500 to conduct the security test.

stories.title: Run AI Coding Agents Safely with Docker Sandboxes
stories.author: pradumnasaraf
bullet_points: The article addresses the security risks associated with running AI coding agents.
It proposes using Docker sandboxes as a method to safely execute these agents.
The core problem involves preventing untrusted AI agents from executing harmful commands or modifying systems.

stories.title: Why AI Agents Fail in Production (And How Engineering Teams Are Fixing It in 2026)
stories.author: hadil
bullet_points: The article addresses common failure points in production AI agents.
It posits that infrastructure, rather than model quality, is the primary cause of agent failures.
It discusses engineering solutions being implemented to improve AI agent reliability by 2026.

stories.title: I Did the Math on GitHub Copilot's New AI Credits Billing. The 24x Price Gap Changes Everything.
stories.author: tokenmixai
bullet_points: GitHub Copilot is transitioning its billing model from Premium Request Units to usage-based AI Credits starting June 1, 2026.
The author analyzed the cost differences across 10 models and 5 real workflows, highlighting significant price variations.
The analysis reveals a substantial price gap, with the same agent run costing between $0.0068 and $1.85 depending on the chosen model.
